element_introduction_order.element: Basic flight controls and cockpit HUD
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial dogfight or initial exploration in starting system
Player must defeat or evade initial threats to reach landing zones
Dialogue trees and fixer missions introduce faction mechanics

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early gameplay, reinforced through optional merchant gameplay
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Profit-seeking loops via price arbitrage between systems
Player actions determine faction stance; some factions attack on sight if reputation poor

element_introduction_order.element: Mission computer and random non-plot missions
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Repeatable income source; allows skill building before plot advancement
Sector traversal opens map to player choice
Story gateway; motivates exploration and reveals Steltek lore

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid-game revelation with Dr. Monkhouse on Palan
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Powerful weapon acquisition; mysterious drone becomes major threat
High-stakes combat coordination required to destroy drone
Homicidal extremists with Steltek weapon copies; climactic antagonist

typical_relief_triggers: Safe landing at base or planet
typical_tension_triggers: Entering combat zone with hostile faction
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sandbox-style with player-controlled rhythm. Linear plot missions punctuate optional exploration and trading loops. Tension scales with mission difficulty choice; player can disengage from threats via safe harbors or mission abort to trading/grinding phases.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Quadrant and system branching structure allows player to explore non-mandatory regions; some systems may be hostile faction strongholds signaling danger
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Ancient shipwrecks contain hidden Steltek artifacts and powerful weapons; exploration of unmarked locations reveals lore and equipment

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction via basic combat and safe trading. Starting ship and credits allow survival of initial drone encounter and early missions. Player can choose mission difficulty via mission computer selection.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation through faction missions and plot involvement. Steltek weapon acquisition raises stakes. Multiple factions become active threats. Smuggling and escort missions introduce environmental hazard (combat + objective protection).
difficulty_curve.late_game: Climactic drone destruction and Steltek scout encounter in base game. Expansion adds Retro faction as persistent antagonist with escalating weapon capabilities.
notable_spikes: Dr. Monkhouse revelation and ancient weapon discovery (mid-game gate)
Confederation ambush mission against drone (high-stakes cooperation)
Righteous Fire: Retro leader Mordecai Jones conflict with stolen weapon recovery

notable_level_patterns.description: Gemini Sector organized into quadrants with multiple interconnected systems. Player discovers planets/bases through jump navigation. Each location is a static hub with NPC interactions, trading, and mission assignment.
Mandatory plot missions coexist with optional merchant trading, random non-plot missions, and faction-specific side work. Player can interrupt plot progression to grind credits or reputation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any system after first base landing

notable_level_patterns.description: First-person 3D cockpit view during flight/combat shifts to static overview and menu-driven interaction on planets/bases. Provides pacing contrast between action and dialogue/commerce.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Flight to any planet followed by landing sequence

notable_level_patterns.description: Certain systems and missions blocked or dangerous based on player reputation with factions (merchants, bounty hunters, retros, pirates, militia, Confederation, Kilrathi). Player must manage reputation or risk hostile encounters.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Retro-controlled systems require low Retro reputation or high Confederation standing
